contact-url.description: The info.contact object is missing a url field. API consumers cannot find the organization's homepage or support portal.
contact-url.fixSuggestion: Add a url field to the info.contact object, e.g. "url": "https://example.com".

contact-email.description: The info.contact object is missing an email field. API consumers have no direct contact address for support.
contact-email.fixSuggestion: Add an email field to the info.contact object, e.g. "email": "support@example.com".

info-contact.description: The info object has no contact section. Consumers cannot determine who owns or supports this API.
info-contact.fixSuggestion: Add an info.contact object with at least name, email, and url fields.

info-description.description: The info object has no description. Consumers and tools cannot understand the API's purpose without reading its paths.
info-description.fixSuggestion: Add an info.description string summarising what the API does and who it is for.

info-license.description: The info object has no license section. Consumers cannot determine the terms under which they may use the API.
info-license.fixSuggestion: Add an info.license object with name and url, e.g. Apache 2.0 or MIT.

license-url.description: The license object is missing a url. Consumers must look up the full license text manually.
license-url.fixSuggestion: Add a url field to info.license pointing to the canonical license text.

no-eval-in-markdown.description: A description field contains eval() which is a JavaScript injection vector. Documentation renderers that execute scripts may be exploited.
no-eval-in-markdown.fixSuggestion: Remove or escape the eval() call in the affected description field.

no-script-tags-in-markdown.description: A description field contains a <script> tag, which can execute arbitrary JavaScript in documentation renderers.
no-script-tags-in-markdown.fixSuggestion: Remove or escape the <script> tag from the affected description field.

openapi-tags-alphabetical.description: Global tags are not in alphabetical order, making the tag list harder to scan in generated documentation.
openapi-tags-alphabetical.fixSuggestion: Sort the root-level tags array alphabetically by the name property.

openapi-tags.description: The spec has no global tags array. Operations cannot be grouped in documentation and discovery tools.
openapi-tags.fixSuggestion: Add a root-level tags array with at least one entry, each having a name and description.

operation-description.description: One or more operations have no description. Consumers cannot understand what the operation does beyond its HTTP method and path.
operation-description.fixSuggestion: Add a non-empty description string to each operation explaining its purpose and behaviour.

operation-operationId.description: One or more operations are missing an operationId. Code generators, SDKs, and documentation tools rely on operationId to name methods.
operation-operationId.fixSuggestion: Add a unique operationId to each operation using kebab-case or camelCase, e.g. list-users or getOrder.

operation-operationId-valid-in-url.description: An operationId contains characters that are invalid in URLs. This breaks SDK generators, deep-link URLs, and tool integrations.
operation-operationId-valid-in-url.fixSuggestion: Replace invalid characters (spaces, pipes, brackets, etc.) in the operationId with hyphens or underscores.

operation-tags.description: One or more operations have no tags. Documentation tools cannot group or filter operations without tags.
operation-tags.fixSuggestion: Add a non-empty tags array to each operation referencing a tag defined in the global tags list.

path-declarations-must-exist.description: A path contains an empty parameter placeholder {} which is not a valid path parameter. Routers and validators will reject or misparse the path.
path-declarations-must-exist.fixSuggestion: Replace {} with a named parameter such as {userId}.

paths-no-trailing-slash.description: A path ends with a trailing slash. /users and /users/ are treated as different resources, causing inconsistent routing and client confusion.
paths-no-trailing-slash.fixSuggestion: Remove the trailing slash from the affected path, e.g. change /users/ to /users.

contact-name.description: The contact object is missing a name field. Consumers cannot identify the team or organisation responsible for the API.
contact-name.fixSuggestion: Add a name field to info.contact, e.g. "name": "API Support Team".

path-not-include-query.description: A path key contains a query string character. Query parameters embedded in paths are not properly parsed by OpenAPI tooling.
path-not-include-query.fixSuggestion: Remove the query string from the path and define the parameter with in: query under parameters.

tag-description.description: A global tag has no description. Documentation tools display the tag without any context about what operations it groups.
tag-description.fixSuggestion: Add a description field to each entry in the root-level tags array.

parameter-description.description: One or more parameters have no description. Consumers cannot understand what value to provide or its expected format.
parameter-description.fixSuggestion: Add a description string to each parameter object explaining its purpose, format, and valid values.

oas3-examples-value-or-externalValue.description: An example object has both value and externalValue set, or neither. The OpenAPI spec requires exactly one.
oas3-examples-value-or-externalValue.fixSuggestion: Keep either value (inline example) or externalValue (URL to example), and remove the other.

array-items.description: An array-type schema is missing its items field. Validators and code generators cannot determine the element type.
array-items.fixSuggestion: Add an items field to every schema with type: array describing the element's type and constraints.

path-parameters-on-path-only.description: Path parameters are defined at the operation level instead of the path level. This leads to duplication and inconsistency across operations on the same path.
path-parameters-on-path-only.fixSuggestion: Move path parameters from individual operations up to the path-level parameters array so they apply to all operations on that path.

paths-no-query-params.description: A path key contains query parameters embedded in the URL string. These are invisible to OpenAPI tooling and cannot be validated.
paths-no-query-params.fixSuggestion: Remove query parameters from the path string and define them as parameters with in: query.

path-casing.description: A path segment uses non-kebab-case naming (e.g. camelCase, PascalCase, or underscores). Inconsistent casing makes URLs harder to read and breaks case-sensitive routing.
path-casing.fixSuggestion: Rename path segments to kebab-case, e.g. change /userInfo to /user-info.

resource-names-plural.description: A resource name in the path appears to be singular. REST conventions use plural nouns for collection resources (e.g. /users, not /user).
resource-names-plural.fixSuggestion: Rename the path segment to a plural noun, e.g. change /user to /users.

paths-no-http-verbs.description: A path contains an HTTP verb (get, post, delete, etc.) in the URL. This duplicates information already conveyed by the HTTP method and is an anti-pattern in RESTful design.
paths-no-http-verbs.fixSuggestion: Remove the verb from the path and express the action through the HTTP method, e.g. change /getUsers to /users with GET.

paths-avoid-special-characters.description: A path contains special characters such as $ & + , ; = ? @%. These characters have reserved meanings in URLs and cause encoding issues.
paths-avoid-special-characters.fixSuggestion: Remove special characters from path segments. Use hyphens or underscores if word separation is needed.
